narration_text: Now we'll see how packets are handled in a router, let's take a look at the diagram on the slide, notice how packets are queued in the buffer, waiting for their turn to be transmitted. The queue length grows when the arrival rate exceeds the output link capacity, and packet loss occurs when the buffer is full. I want you to focus on the image, observe how the packets are lined up, and think about what happens when the buffer is overloaded. This visual representation helps us understand the relationship between packet arrival rate and link capacity. As we analyze this further, we'll see how it affects network performance, and this concept will be crucial in understanding the next topic we'll be covering.
